english: par L. Feer Paris 1866), but afterwards it came under Mahometan rulers, and k'a-čé (kha che) denotes therefore now in C. a mussulman (cf. Huc & Gabet's journey); k'a-čéi dpé-c'a (kha che'i dpe cha) the koran Schr.; k'a-čéi ˳grón-k'aṅ (kha che'i mgron khang) an inn kept by a mussulman Mil.
(k'va-ta?) Ssk. 1. crow. — 2. raven, = bya-róg (bya rog), p'o-róg (pho rog). — 3. k'a-ta k'rá-bo (kha ta khra bo) magpie.
is said to be = gsál-šiṅ (gsal shing), a pointed stake used for the execution of criminals.
k'a-ṭv. (kha Tw.), gen. pronounced k'a-ṭóm-ga (kha Tom ga) Ssk., Will.: 'a club or staff with a skull at the top', the weapon of Siva, also carried by ascetics; Tibetans refer it also to the trident.

handkerchief or scarf of salutation, a piece of veil-like and generally worthless silk-fabric, about as large as a small pocket-handkerchief, which in Tibet is given or sent, with or without other presents, to the person one intends to visit; cf. Huc's journey.
v. k'an-da (khan da).

I. col. C. *k'á-po* (*kha po*), W. *k'án-te* (*khan te*), Bal. *χo* (*kho*) bitter. — II. W. *k'a* (*kha*) snow, k'á-ba duṅ ltar gsál (kha ba dung ltar gsal) bright as snow and shells Pth.; k'á-ba ˳bab (kha ba 'bab), col. *k'a yoṅ* (*kha yong*) it snows; *k'a páṅ-če* (*kha pang che*) W. to remove the snow (with a shovel); k'á-ba-čan (kha ba can) snowy, and as a subst.: the snow-country, Tibet; k'á-ba-čan-pai séms-čan-rnams (kha ba can pa'i sems can rnams) the Tibetan beings Glr. — III. correspondently to the Arabian word قهوة the missionaries in Lh. have given to k'a-ba (kha ba) the signification of coffee, which is otherwise unknown in Tibet.
1. the architectural ornament of a Tibetan house formed by the projecting ends of the beams which support the roof (not 'parapet' Cs.) — 2. v. k'a (kha).
fork (not generally used in eating); any forked object.
Cs. enchantment, irresistible influence.
fringes, threads, such as the loose threads at the end of a web.
Sch. decision; but in the only passage where I met with this word, viz. Dzl. 2SV 13, this meaning is not applicable, but something like surface or width.
Sch. water-hen.
(Ssk. खर्जूर, Hindi खजूर) col. k'a-zúr-pa-ni (kha zur pa ni) date, k'a-zur šiṅ (kha zur shing) date-tree.
1. W.: rake (gardening). — 2. Sp.: a carrier's load, *k'a-zé-pa* (*kha ze pa*) a cooly.
a false charge, C.: *ma nyé-pe k'a-yóg ˳j'uṅ* (*ma nye pe kha yog byung*) he was innocently accused.
1. W. f k'á-ra (kha ra) sugar. — 2. Sch.: trough, manger.
or k'a-rú (kha ru), v. k'al-ri (khal ri).
v. k'a rog-pa (kha rog pa), k'a (kha) IV. 1. towards the end.
v. k'yá-le (khya le).

1. v. k'a (kha) Comp. — 2. Schr. prow of a ship, others: helm; the word is very often used in the phrase: k'a-lo sgyur-ba (kha lo sgyur ba), esp. gru-rziṅs-kyi (gru rzings kyi), to turn a ship, to steer, to lead, govern, rule, k'a-lo sgyur-mk'as-pa (kha lo sgyur mkhas pa) skilful in driving, k'a-lo-pa (kha lo pa) a charioteer. — 3. Cs.: the glans penis.
n. of a mountainous country in the N.E. of India Tar. 21. 10.
v. k'a-zur (kha zur).

1. a task; charge, business, duty; responsibility; importance; k'ag k'úr-ba (khag khur ba) to be charged with, k'ág ˳gél-ba (khag 'gel ba) to lay upon; *k'ag t'ég-pa* (*khag theg pa*) or *k'yág-pa* (*khyag pa*) C. to warrant, become responsible; dér ˳ts'ó-ba yóṅ-ba k'ag t'eg (der 'tsho ba yong ba khag theg) I warrant you will get something to eat there Mil.; *k'ag -t'ég, k'ag-k'yág* (*khag theg, khag khyag*) C. a bail; k'ág-čan (khag can) important. — 2. W.: part, bču-k'ág (bcu khag) the tenth part, tithe, *k'ag-nyi č'o-té čád-če* (*khag nyi cho te cad ce*) to cut in two; division, section (of a book); place, *k'ág nyi-la ˳p'og soṅ* (*khag nyi la phog song*) I have hurt myself in two places; *k'ag číg-la rúb-če* (*khag cig la rub ce*) to press towards one point; in a more general sense: *k'ag číg-la 'i
